mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to yield positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output and pricing power, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth yields negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during tightening periods, or the signal decays within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.8% monthly yield positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or inflation surprises show no predictive power for equity direction over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with corporate earnings expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals yield negative 5-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the correlation between GDPC1 changes and SPX direction falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside falling retail sales signals a synchronized demand-supply contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in industrial production and retail sales fail to predict negative 20-day returns in >55% of late-cycle observations, or the joint signal loses predictive accuracy.
